title: 你好 (Hello)
subtitle: Greetings
description: Learn basic Chinese greetings and simple introductions.

grammar_notes.title: Greetings with 你好
grammar_notes.explanation: 你好 (nǐ hǎo) is the most common Chinese greeting, used at any time of day. Literally 'you good'.

title: 你忙吗 (Are You Busy?)
subtitle: Asking About Wellbeing
description: Ask how someone is doing and respond about your own state.

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.pinyin: Nǐ hǎo, Lù Yǔpíng. Nǐ máng ma?
dialogue.english: Hello, Lu Yuping. Are you busy?

grammar_notes.title: Yes/No questions with 吗
grammar_notes.explanation: Add 吗 (ma) at the end of a statement to turn it into a yes/no question. Example: 你忙 (you busy) → 你忙吗? (Are you busy?)

grammar_notes.title: Negation with 不
grammar_notes.explanation: 不 (bù) negates verbs and adjectives. Example: 我忙 (I am busy) → 我不忙 (I am not busy).

title: 她是哪国人 (Where Is She From?)
subtitle: Nationality and Identity
description: Talk about people, nationalities, and ask 'who' questions.

grammar_notes.title: 是 (shì) — the verb 'to be'
grammar_notes.explanation: 是 connects two nouns. Structure: A 是 B (A is B). Example: 他是中国人 (He is Chinese).

grammar_notes.title: Question word 哪 (nǎ)
grammar_notes.explanation: 哪 means 'which' and forms questions: 哪国人 = 'which country person' = 'what nationality'.

title: 认识你很高兴 (Nice to Meet You)
subtitle: Introductions
description: Introduce yourself, ask names, and express pleasure in meeting someone.

dialogue.speaker: Ma Dawei
dialogue.pinyin: Wǒ jiào Mǎ Dàwéi. Nǐ ne?
dialogue.english: My name is Ma Dawei. And you?

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.chinese: 我叫林娜。认识你很高兴。
dialogue.pinyin: Wǒ jiào Lín Nà. Rènshí nǐ hěn gāoxìng.
dialogue.english: My name is Lin Na. Nice to meet you.

dialogue.speaker: Ma Dawei
dialogue.pinyin: Rènshí nǐ wǒ yě hěn gāoxìng.

grammar_notes.title: 叫 (jiào) — to be called
grammar_notes.explanation: 叫 expresses one's name. 你叫什么名字? = What is your name? Response: 我叫... = I am called...

grammar_notes.title: 什么 (shénme) — what
grammar_notes.explanation: 什么 is a question word for 'what'. Place it where the answer would appear: 你叫什么? = You called what?

title: 餐厅在哪儿 (Where Is the Restaurant?)
subtitle: Locations and Directions
description: Ask and give directions, discuss locations of places.

dialogue.speaker: Ma Dawei
dialogue.english: Excuse me, where is the restaurant?

grammar_notes.title: 在 (zài) — to be at/in
grammar_notes.explanation: 在 indicates location. Structure: [Subject] + 在 + [Place]. Example: 我在家 (I am at home).

grammar_notes.title: 哪儿 (nǎr) — where
grammar_notes.explanation: 哪儿 asks for location. 餐厅在哪儿? = Where is the restaurant?

title: 我们去游泳，好吗 (Let's Go Swimming)
subtitle: Making Suggestions
description: Suggest activities, ask for opinions, and accept invitations.

dialogue.speaker: Wang Xiaoyun
dialogue.english: The weather is really nice today.

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.pinyin: Shì a. Wǒmen qù yóuyǒng, hǎo ma?

dialogue.speaker: Wang Xiaoyun
dialogue.chinese: 太好了!我们什么时候去?
dialogue.pinyin: Tài hǎo le! Wǒmen shénme shíhòu qù?

grammar_notes.title: Suggestions with ...，好吗?
grammar_notes.explanation: Add ，好吗? after a statement to make a suggestion: 我们去看电影，好吗? (Shall we go to a movie?)

grammar_notes.title: Telling Time
grammar_notes.explanation: Use number + 点 for hours: 三点 (3 o'clock), 八点 (8 o'clock). 下午 = afternoon, 上午 = morning.

title: 你认识不认识他 (Do You Know Him?)
subtitle: V-not-V Questions
description: Form questions by repeating the verb with 不 between.

dialogue.speaker: Ma Dawei
dialogue.pinyin: Lín Nà, nǐ rènshí bú rènshí tā?
dialogue.english: Lin Na, do you know him?

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.pinyin: Wǒ bú rènshí tā. Tā shì shéi?
dialogue.english: I don't know him. Who is he?

dialogue.speaker: Ma Dawei
dialogue.chinese: 他是我的同学，叫王小云。
dialogue.pinyin: Tā shì wǒ de tóngxué, jiào Wáng Xiǎoyún.

grammar_notes.title: V-not-V Questions
grammar_notes.explanation: Repeat the verb with 不 to make a question: 你认识不认识他? = 你认识他吗? (Do you know him?). 你忙不忙? = Are you busy?

title: 你们家有几口人 (How Many in Your Family?)
subtitle: Family & Numbers
description: Discuss family members and use measure words for people.

dialogue.speaker: Wang Xiaoyun
dialogue.pinyin: Lín Nà, nǐmen jiā yǒu jǐ kǒu rén?
dialogue.english: Lin Na, how many people are in your family?

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.chinese: 我们家有四口人。爸爸、妈妈、哥哥和我。
dialogue.pinyin: Wǒmen jiā yǒu sì kǒu rén. Bàba, māma, gēge hé wǒ.
dialogue.english: We have four people: dad, mom, older brother, and me.

grammar_notes.title: 有 (yǒu) — to have
grammar_notes.explanation: 有 means 'to have' or 'there is/are'. Negation: 没有 (méi yǒu). Example: 我有一个哥哥 (I have an older brother).

grammar_notes.title: 几 (jǐ) — how many (small numbers)
grammar_notes.explanation: 几 asks 'how many' for small numbers (usually <10). 你有几本书? = How many books do you have?

title: 他今年二十岁 (He's Twenty This Year)
subtitle: Numbers & Ages
description: Talk about ages, birthdays, and use larger numbers.

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.pinyin: Mǎ Dàwéi, nǐ jīnnián duō dà?
dialogue.english: Ma Dawei, how old are you this year?

dialogue.speaker: Ma Dawei
dialogue.pinyin: Wǒ jīnnián èrshí suì. Nǐ ne?
dialogue.english: I'm twenty this year. And you?

grammar_notes.title: Asking Age: 多大?
grammar_notes.explanation: 你今年多大? = How old are you this year? (for adults). For children: 你几岁? Response: 我...岁.

title: 我在这儿买光盘 (I'm Buying CDs Here)
subtitle: Shopping & Activities
description: Express what you are doing now using 在 + verb.

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.pinyin: Mǎ Dàwéi, nǐ zài zuò shénme?
dialogue.english: Ma Dawei, what are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.english: What kind of CDs do you want to buy?

grammar_notes.title: 在 + Verb (progressive)
grammar_notes.explanation: 在 before a verb expresses an ongoing action: 我在吃饭 (I am eating). 你在做什么? = What are you doing?

grammar_notes.title: 想 (xiǎng) — want to
grammar_notes.explanation: 想 + verb expresses desire: 我想买书 (I want to buy a book). 你想去吗? = Do you want to go?

title: 我会说一点儿汉语 (I Can Speak a Little Chinese)
subtitle: Abilities & Languages
description: Talk about what you can do using 会 and 能.

dialogue.speaker: Ma Dawei
dialogue.english: I can speak a little Chinese.

grammar_notes.title: 会 (huì) — learned ability
grammar_notes.explanation: 会 expresses learned skills: 我会游泳 (I can swim). Negation: 我不会...

grammar_notes.title: 一点儿 (yìdiǎnr) — a little
grammar_notes.explanation: 一点儿 means 'a little'. 我会说一点儿英语 = I can speak a little English.

title: 我全身都不舒服 (I'm Not Feeling Well)
subtitle: Health & Body
description: Describe how you feel and discuss being sick.

dialogue.speaker: Ma Dawei
dialogue.chinese: 我全身都不舒服。头疼，发烧。
dialogue.pinyin: Wǒ quánshēn dōu bù shūfu. Tóu téng, fāshāo.
dialogue.english: My whole body feels bad. My head hurts and I have a fever.

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.english: You should go to the hospital.

grammar_notes.title: 怎么了? (zěnme le?)
grammar_notes.explanation: 怎么了? asks 'what's wrong?' or 'what happened?'. Used to inquire about a problem.

grammar_notes.title: 应该 (yīnggāi) — should
grammar_notes.explanation: 应该 + verb expresses obligation: 你应该休息 (You should rest).

title: 我认识了一个漂亮的姑娘 (I Met a Beautiful Girl)
subtitle: Past Actions with 了
description: Talk about completed actions using the particle 了.

dialogue.speaker: Ma Dawei
dialogue.chinese: 昨天我认识了一个漂亮的姑娘。
dialogue.pinyin: Zuótiān wǒ rènshí le yí ge piàoliang de gūniang.
dialogue.english: Yesterday I met a beautiful girl.

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.pinyin: Zhēn de ma? Tā shì nǎ guó rén?

dialogue.speaker: Ma Dawei
dialogue.chinese: 她是中国人。她叫王小云。
dialogue.pinyin: Tā shì Zhōngguó rén. Tā jiào Wáng Xiǎoyún.
dialogue.english: She's Chinese. Her name is Wang Xiaoyun.

grammar_notes.title: 了 (le) — completed action
grammar_notes.explanation: 了 after a verb indicates a completed action: 我吃了饭 (I ate). Past time markers like 昨天 (yesterday) often appear with 了.

grammar_notes.title: Adjective + 的 + Noun
grammar_notes.explanation: Use 的 to link adjectives to nouns: 漂亮的姑娘 (beautiful girl), 好的朋友 (good friend).

title: 祝你圣诞快乐 (Merry Christmas)
subtitle: Wishes & Celebrations
description: Express good wishes and talk about holidays.

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.pinyin: Mǎ Dàwéi, zhù nǐ Shèngdàn kuàilè!

dialogue.speaker: Ma Dawei
dialogue.pinyin: Xièxie! Yě zhù nǐ Shèngdàn kuàilè!
dialogue.english: Thank you! Merry Christmas to you too!

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.pinyin: Zhè shì gěi nǐ de lǐwù.
dialogue.english: This is a gift for you.

grammar_notes.title: 祝 (zhù) — to wish
grammar_notes.explanation: 祝 + Person + Wish expresses good wishes: 祝你生日快乐 (Happy birthday), 祝你健康 (Wishing you health).

grammar_notes.title: 给 (gěi) — for; give
grammar_notes.explanation: 给 + Person + 的 + Noun: 给你的礼物 (a gift for you). Also a verb: 我给他书 (I give him a book).

title: 银行在哪儿 (Where Is the Bank?)
subtitle: Getting Around Town
description: Ask for directions to common city locations.

dialogue.speaker: Ma Dawei
dialogue.english: Excuse me, where is the bank?

dialogue.speaker: Stranger
dialogue.english: The bank is next to the post office.

dialogue.speaker: Stranger
dialogue.pinyin: Bù yuǎn, zǒu wǔ fēnzhōng jiù dào.

grammar_notes.title: Location words: 旁边, 前面, 后面
grammar_notes.explanation: 旁边 (beside), 前面 (in front), 后面 (behind), 里面 (inside), 外面 (outside). A 在 B 旁边 = A is beside B.

title: 你想喝什么 (What Do You Want to Drink?)
subtitle: Ordering Food & Drink
description: Order at a restaurant and discuss preferences.

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.english: What would you like to drink?

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.english: I'd like a cup of tea.

grammar_notes.title: 要 (yào) — want; will
grammar_notes.explanation: 要 expresses wanting or ordering: 我要茶 (I want tea). Also used as future: 我要去 (I will go).

grammar_notes.title: Measure words: 杯, 个, 本
grammar_notes.explanation: Chinese requires measure words between numbers and nouns: 一杯茶 (a cup of tea), 一个人 (one person), 一本书 (one book).

title: 这件衣服多少钱 (How Much Is This?)
subtitle: Shopping & Prices
description: Ask about prices and shop for clothing.

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.english: How much is this piece of clothing?

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.pinyin: Tài guì le. Piányi yìdiǎnr ba.
dialogue.english: Too expensive. A bit cheaper, please.

grammar_notes.title: 多少 (duōshǎo) — how much; how many
grammar_notes.explanation: 多少 asks for amounts (especially large): 多少钱? (how much money?), 多少人? (how many people?)

grammar_notes.title: 太...了 — too ...
grammar_notes.explanation: 太 + adjective + 了 expresses 'too...': 太贵了 (too expensive), 太好了 (great/too good).

title: 我每天七点起床 (I Get Up at 7 Every Day)
subtitle: Daily Routines
description: Describe your daily schedule and routines.

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.english: What time do you get up every day?

dialogue.speaker: Ma Dawei
dialogue.pinyin: Wǒ měitiān qī diǎn qǐchuáng. Nǐ ne?
dialogue.english: I get up at 7 every day. And you?

grammar_notes.title: 每 (měi) — every
grammar_notes.explanation: 每 + time expresses 'every': 每天 (every day), 每年 (every year), 每个星期 (every week).

title: 今天比昨天冷 (Today Is Colder Than Yesterday)
subtitle: Comparisons
description: Compare two things using 比.

dialogue.speaker: Ma Dawei
dialogue.pinyin: Shì a. Wǒ xǐhuān rè tiān.

grammar_notes.title: A 比 B + Adjective
grammar_notes.explanation: Use 比 to compare: 我比他高 (I am taller than him). 今天比昨天冷 = Today is colder than yesterday.

grammar_notes.title: 喜欢 (xǐhuān) — to like
grammar_notes.explanation: 喜欢 + Noun/Verb: 我喜欢中国 (I like China), 我喜欢学汉语 (I like to study Chinese).

title: 我打算去中国 (I Plan to Go to China)
subtitle: Plans & Future
description: Talk about plans and future actions.

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.chinese: 明年我打算去中国学习。
dialogue.pinyin: Míngnián wǒ dǎsuàn qù Zhōngguó xuéxí.
dialogue.english: Next year I plan to go study in China.

dialogue.speaker: Ma Dawei
dialogue.chinese: 真的吗?你要去哪个城市?
dialogue.pinyin: Zhēn de ma? Nǐ yào qù nǎ ge chéngshì?
dialogue.english: Really? Which city will you go to?

dialogue.speaker: Lin Na
dialogue.pinyin: Běijīng. Nǐ xiǎng yìqǐ lái ma?
dialogue.english: Beijing. Do you want to come along?

grammar_notes.title: 打算 (dǎsuàn) — to plan
grammar_notes.explanation: 打算 + verb: 我打算学汉语 (I plan to study Chinese). Talks about intentions or plans.

grammar_notes.title: 一起 (yìqǐ) — together
grammar_notes.explanation: 一起 + verb: 一起去 (go together), 我们一起吃饭 (Let's eat together).
